compiled_state.title: Scaffold RED eval for red-test-writer runtime budget
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0)
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] ruff check passes (exit 0)
  Verify: ruff check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py
- [ ] ruff format --check passes (exit 0)
  Verify: ruff format --check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py
- [ ] Eval config file exists and is valid YAML
  Verify: python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('evals/red-test-writer/promptfooconfig.yaml'))"
- [ ] Eval contains at least one scenario with known-slow operation (subprocess/heavy I/O)
  Verify: python3 -c "import yaml; c=yaml.safe_load(open('evals/red-test-writer/promptfooconfig.yaml')); assert any('slow' in str(t).lower() or 'subprocess' in str(t).lower() or 'sleep' in str(t).lower() for t in c.get('tests', []))"
- [ ] Eval uses llm-rubric assertion type targeting ESTIMATED_RUNTIME_RED presence and restructure-or-reject behavior
  Verify: grep -q "llm-rubric" evals/red-test-writer/promptfooconfig.yaml
- [ ] Running eval against current (unmodified) agent confirms RED (assertion fails)
  Verify: manual — run eval, confirm assertion failure

## TDD Role
Creates RED test. Establishes failing assertion before implementation (Task 2).

## Files
- Create: evals/red-test-writer/promptfooconfig.yaml

comments.body: Fixed: RED eval scaffolded with 4 test scenarios (subprocess, sleep, heavy I/O, network). All AC verified. Completion verifier PASS.
comments.author: Main
